    • Multi-modal Analysis: Cell-Graph Features with Genomic and Clinical Data
    • EDA: Graph-based Features from Spatial Cell Data

Build a spatial igraph from a nuclei list

Source: R/plotGraph.R
buildSpatialGraph.Rd

Constructs one of five spatial graph types (Delaunay, k-NN, MST, Gabriel, or RNG) from a list of nucleus objects and returns an igraph with x, y, and type vertex attributes. The resulting object can be passed directly to plotGraph.

Usage

buildSpatialGraph(
  nuclei_list,
  type = c("delaunay", "knn", "mst", "gabriel", "rng"),
  k = 5L
)

Arguments

nuclei_list

A list of nucleus objects. Each element must contain:

centroid

Numeric vector of length 2 (x, y coordinates).

type

Character scalar — cell-type label.

type

Character(1). Graph type to build. One of "delaunay", "knn", "mst", "gabriel", or "rng".

k

Integer(1). Number of nearest neighbours for type = "knn". Ignored for all other types. Default 5L.

Value

An undirected igraph with vertex attributes x, y (centroid coordinates) and type (cell-type label).

Examples

set.seed(42)
nuclei <- lapply(seq_len(30), function(i)
    list(centroid = runif(2, 0, 500),
         type     = sample(c("neoplastic", "stromal", "immune"), 1)))

g_del <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei, type = "delaunay")
g_knn <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei, type = "knn", k = 5)
g_mst <- buildSpatialGraph(nuclei, type = "mst")
